design_philosophy.core_loop: Navigate side-scrolling levels as a skateboarding dinosaur, using roar, fire breath, and bubble spray abilities to defeat enemies and reach the goal
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing fast skateboard momentum while collecting power-ups and avoiding obstacles; balancing approachable 'cute' aesthetic with functional platforming challenges
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing as a radical, fire-breathing skateboarding T-Rex on a mission to rescue girlfriend Rexanne from evil magician Sethron
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Commits to 90s 'extreme attitude' branding and anthropomorphic dinosaur protagonist, prioritizing character charm over gameplay innovation; deliberately pitched at younger/family audience with cutesy humorous graphics despite some difficult design elements

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Hidden level select accessible via Controller 2 input sequence (Right, A, Down, Right, Y, X) provides player option to skip ahead; reviewers noted game is 'too easy' for older players but appropriately challenging for target younger demographic

progression.unlock_mechanism: Hidden level select available for sequence completion or controller code entry
replayability_hooks: Multiple platform versions (SNES, Genesis, Sega CD) with version-specific changes
Boss character substitution across platforms (Sethron on SNES replaced by weasel Skriitch on Genesis/Sega CD)
New levels and enemies in Sega CD version

world_building_techniques: Opening rap sequence (SNES version) establishes tone and character
Simple premise: rescue girlfriend from evil magician
Enemy variety suggests dinosaur/prehistoric world under magical threat (dinosaurs, sea creatures, monsters)

boss_design.philosophy: Frustratingly difficult bosses contrasting with relatively approachable main platforming; boss difficulty noted as punishing relative to level design
notable_bosses: Sethron (SNES) / Skriitch (Genesis/Sega CD) - primary antagonist, weasel-like mammal in later versions

faction_indicators: Good: Radical Rex (skateboarding hero, fire-breathing protector)
Evil: Sethron/Skriitch (magician/weasel antagonist threatening the land)
danger_signals: Kidnapping of girlfriend Rexanne by evil magician signals threat to social order
Invasion by dinosaurs, sea creatures, and monsters suggests environmental/magical destabilization
environmental_storytelling: Murky background graphics (criticized) suggest corrupted or darkened world
Mix of prehistoric dinosaurs and fantasy monsters implies magical corruption of natural order
Skateboard protagonist in dinosaur world creates incongruous aesthetic tension suggesting displaced/radical disruption

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Radical Rex (SNES) article
